memory: When canonical state files lag behind append-only team evidence, continuity must not silently treat the stale canonical snapshot as current truth. The durable interpretation is layered: canonical files define baseline governance; later source-bound queue updates define observed progress and active unresolved work until an explicit reconciliation artifact promotes them into canonical state.

observed: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json identifies M-001 as the only continuity item and marks it completed.
A later committed queue update identifies M-RECONCILE-002 as active and records runtime-verified progress plus remaining work.
The canonical queue timestamp predates the later continuity commits.
inferred: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is a stale baseline for continuity ownership, not sufficient by itself to select the current highest-priority continuity item.
M-RECONCILE-002 is the current active continuity item represented by the newest source-bound queue-update evidence inspected in this run.
proposed: Add a reconciliation process that computes effective queue state from canonical baseline plus append-only queue updates, while retaining both layers and their timestamps.
Require any future canonical queue refresh to cite the exact queue-update records it incorporates.
superseded: Selecting continuity work solely from the latest contents of operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json when newer committed queue-update evidence exists.
unresolved: No canonical reconciliation artifact currently proves that every append-only queue update has been incorporated.
Authenticated exhaustive repository traversal and origin classification for M-004, M-005, and M-006 remain incomplete.

privacy_boundary: Public repository governance files and commit metadata only; no private chat, health, credentials, or identifying personal material included.
falsification_route: Produce a newer explicit canonical reconciliation artifact showing that M-RECONCILE-002 was incorporated, superseded, or closed, or show that the cited queue-update commit is not reachable from the authoritative repository history.
